## Leveraging Preferences in Deep RL

How can we tractably learn a reward $\hat{R_{\theta}}$ *consistent with preferences* over robot behaviors?
\begin{align}
\sum_{(s,~a)~\in~\tau_1} \hat{R}_{\theta}(s,a) > \sum_{(s,~a)~\in~\tau_2} \hat{R}_{\theta}(s,a) \implies \tau_1 \succ \tau_2 
\end{align}

<div style="display: flex; gap: 1em; margin-bottom: -1.0em;">
<div style="flex: 1; font-size: 0.85em;">

1. Optimize $\pi$ for $\hat{R}$ using deep RL
2. Rollout $\pi$ to produce trajectory set $\{\tau_1, \dots, \tau_i\}$.
3. Select trajectory segments $(\sigma_1, \sigma_2)$ and query human for preference.
4. Optimize $\hat{R}$ to be consistent with those preferences.
</div>
<div style="flex: 1;">

![](images/rlhf.png){width="100%"}

</div>
</div>

[@rlhf]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}.
